title: AP World History: Modern MCQ Practice Exam 10

questions.prompt: Originalized legal order, Kamakura Japan, c. 1230: “A warrior who receives land from the shogun must answer the summons, guard the roads, and settle disputes according to precedent. Estate stewards shall not seize temple fields without judgment.” What was the most likely purpose of the order?

choices.A: To replace warrior service with industrial wage labor
choices.B: To define the duties of vassals and protect the shogunate’s authority over land disputes
choices.C: To abolish all Buddhist estates in Japan
choices.D: To place Japan under direct rule by Chinese officials

questions.correctAnswerText: To define the duties of vassals and protect the shogunate’s authority over land disputes
questions.explanation: The order links land grants, military summons, road guarding, legal precedent, and dispute settlement.

questions.prompt: Originalized legal order, Kamakura Japan, c. 1230: “A warrior who receives land from the shogun must answer the summons, guard the roads, and settle disputes according to precedent. Estate stewards shall not seize temple fields without judgment.” Which phrase best supports the claim that landholding was tied to military obligation?

choices.A: “Temple fields”
choices.B: “According to precedent”
choices.C: “Receives land from the shogun must answer the summons”
choices.D: “Without judgment”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Receives land from the shogun must answer the summons”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly connects landholding to military service.

questions.prompt: Originalized legal order, Kamakura Japan, c. 1230: “A warrior who receives land from the shogun must answer the summons, guard the roads, and settle disputes according to precedent. Estate stewards shall not seize temple fields without judgment.” Which broader context best explains the reference to temple fields?

choices.A: Buddhist institutions held land and remained important in Japanese society.
choices.B: Japanese rulers eliminated all religious estates after 1200.
choices.C: Temple landholding was unique to Atlantic plantation colonies.
choices.D: Buddhist institutions appeared in Japan only after 1900.

questions.correctAnswerText: Buddhist institutions held land and remained important in Japanese society.
questions.explanation: Temple fields show that religious institutions possessed land and required legal protection.

questions.prompt: Originalized legal order, Kamakura Japan, c. 1230: “A warrior who receives land from the shogun must answer the summons, guard the roads, and settle disputes according to precedent. Estate stewards shall not seize temple fields without judgment.” The order most directly suggests which feature of Kamakura governance?

choices.A: Complete separation of military elites from land administration
choices.B: Rule by merchant assemblies without warrior authority
choices.C: The disappearance of legal precedent in Japanese politics
choices.D: A warrior government that used law, land grants, and service obligations to maintain order

questions.correctAnswerText: A warrior government that used law, land grants, and service obligations to maintain order
questions.explanation: The order reflects shogunal governance through legal rules, landholding, and warrior service.

questions.prompt: Secondary-source description of Tenochtitlan, c. 1450: “A city built on lake islands contained causeways, canals, marketplaces, temples, and palaces. Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food collected from surrounding tribute provinces.” Which interpretation is best supported by the description?

choices.A: Tenochtitlan was a small village isolated from regional exchange.
choices.B: The Mexica state rejected urban planning and market activity.
choices.C: Tenochtitlan combined urban infrastructure, tribute, markets, and religious-political authority.
choices.D: The city depended on European merchants before 1450.

questions.correctAnswerText: Tenochtitlan combined urban infrastructure, tribute, markets, and religious-political authority.
questions.explanation: The description includes causeways, canals, markets, temples, palaces, tribute goods, and specialized commerce.

questions.prompt: Secondary-source description of Tenochtitlan, c. 1450: “A city built on lake islands contained causeways, canals, marketplaces, temples, and palaces. Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food collected from surrounding tribute provinces.” Which evidence best supports the claim that regional exchange shaped urban life?

choices.A: “Lake islands”
choices.B: “Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food”
choices.C: “Temples and palaces”
choices.D: “Causeways”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food”
questions.explanation: These varied goods indicate regional exchange and market activity.

questions.prompt: Secondary-source description of Tenochtitlan, c. 1450: “A city built on lake islands contained causeways, canals, marketplaces, temples, and palaces. Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food collected from surrounding tribute provinces.” The causeways and canals most directly show which relationship between technology and exchange?

choices.A: Infrastructure helped connect island urban spaces with markets and surrounding regions.
choices.B: Maritime steamships controlled Mesoamerican trade before 1450.
choices.C: Obsidian tools eliminated the need for transportation systems.
choices.D: Urban markets could function only without state-built infrastructure.

questions.correctAnswerText: Infrastructure helped connect island urban spaces with markets and surrounding regions.
questions.explanation: Causeways and canals helped move people and goods through the lake-based city.

questions.prompt: Secondary-source description of Tenochtitlan, c. 1450: “A city built on lake islands contained causeways, canals, marketplaces, temples, and palaces. Merchants sold cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian tools, feathers, and food collected from surrounding tribute provinces.” Which argument is best supported by the description?

choices.A: Mexica trade operated outside political authority.
choices.B: Mesoamerican cities had no specialized goods before 1450.
choices.C: Tribute provinces were unrelated to market exchange.
choices.D: State power and regional exchange could reinforce one another in large American cities.

questions.correctAnswerText: State power and regional exchange could reinforce one another in large American cities.
questions.explanation: The city’s markets, tribute provinces, temples, palaces, and infrastructure show the link between exchange and state power.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Timurid Samarkand c. 1400 shows caravan routes from Iran, India, China, and the steppe converging near madrasas, workshops, and astronomical instruments. Notes say artisans and scholars were brought to the capital after conquest.” Which conclusion is best supported by the map?

choices.A: Samarkand became a center where conquest, scholarship, and exchange networks intersected.
choices.B: Samarkand was isolated from caravan routes.
choices.C: Timurid rulers rejected urban patronage and scholarship.
choices.D: Astronomical knowledge spread only through Atlantic sea routes.

questions.correctAnswerText: Samarkand became a center where conquest, scholarship, and exchange networks intersected.
questions.explanation: Routes, madrasas, workshops, astronomical instruments, and moved specialists all converge in the city.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Timurid Samarkand c. 1400 shows caravan routes from Iran, India, China, and the steppe converging near madrasas, workshops, and astronomical instruments. Notes say artisans and scholars were brought to the capital after conquest.” Which feature best supports the claim that scientific knowledge was cultivated in the city?

choices.A: “The steppe”
choices.B: “Conquest”
choices.C: “Astronomical instruments”
choices.D: “Caravan routes”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Astronomical instruments”
questions.explanation: Astronomical instruments directly indicate scientific and mathematical activity.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Timurid Samarkand c. 1400 shows caravan routes from Iran, India, China, and the steppe converging near madrasas, workshops, and astronomical instruments. Notes say artisans and scholars were brought to the capital after conquest.” Which broader state-building context best explains the pattern shown?

choices.A: Timurid rulers abandoned cities in favor of pastoral isolation.
choices.B: Central Asian rulers avoided patronage of scholars and artisans.
choices.C: Land-based empires had no connection to long-distance trade.
choices.D: Conquest states could use urban patronage and forced movement of skilled people to display power.

questions.correctAnswerText: Conquest states could use urban patronage and forced movement of skilled people to display power.
questions.explanation: Timurid rulers used artisans, scholars, architecture, and urban patronage to enhance imperial prestige.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Timurid Samarkand c. 1400 shows caravan routes from Iran, India, China, and the steppe converging near madrasas, workshops, and astronomical instruments. Notes say artisans and scholars were brought to the capital after conquest.” Which argument is best supported by the description?

choices.A: Timurid power depended entirely on maritime trade.
choices.B: Imperial capitals could concentrate diverse specialists to enhance political prestige.
choices.C: Conquest prevented all cultural production.
choices.D: Scholars in Samarkand had no relationship to imperial authority.

questions.correctAnswerText: Imperial capitals could concentrate diverse specialists to enhance political prestige.
questions.explanation: The map shows artisans and scholars brought to Samarkand and patronized near imperial institutions.

questions.prompt: Originalized Maratha administrative letter, western India, c. 1700: “The hill forts must be stocked with grain, and cavalry captains shall collect chauth from the districts that accept our protection. Village headmen who hide revenue will answer before the minister.” What was the most likely purpose of the letter?

choices.A: To coordinate military defense, tribute collection, and local administration
choices.B: To abolish cavalry service in western India
choices.C: To place Maratha forts under Dutch company rule
choices.D: To end all taxation in the Deccan

questions.correctAnswerText: To coordinate military defense, tribute collection, and local administration
questions.explanation: The letter addresses forts, grain, cavalry, chauth, village headmen, and ministerial authority.

questions.prompt: Originalized Maratha administrative letter, western India, c. 1700: “The hill forts must be stocked with grain, and cavalry captains shall collect chauth from the districts that accept our protection. Village headmen who hide revenue will answer before the minister.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the state depended on revenue extraction?

choices.A: “Hill forts”
choices.B: “Cavalry captains”
choices.C: “Collect chauth”
choices.D: “Accept our protection”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Collect chauth”
questions.explanation: Chauth was a revenue claim, directly showing taxation or tribute extraction.

questions.prompt: Originalized Maratha administrative letter, western India, c. 1700: “The hill forts must be stocked with grain, and cavalry captains shall collect chauth from the districts that accept our protection. Village headmen who hide revenue will answer before the minister.” The reference to hill forts most directly reflects which relationship between geography and power?

choices.A: Coastal ports made inland fortifications unnecessary.
choices.B: Fortified upland positions could support defense and regional control.
choices.C: Hill environments prevented all state formation.
choices.D: Forts were used only by European maritime companies.

questions.correctAnswerText: Fortified upland positions could support defense and regional control.
questions.explanation: Hill forts helped Maratha forces defend territory and project authority in western India.

questions.prompt: Originalized Maratha administrative letter, western India, c. 1700: “The hill forts must be stocked with grain, and cavalry captains shall collect chauth from the districts that accept our protection. Village headmen who hide revenue will answer before the minister.” Which statement best evaluates the political system described?

choices.A: It rejected military service as part of state-building.
choices.B: It treated village headmen as fully independent rulers.
choices.C: It separated military protection from taxation.
choices.D: It linked military authority, local intermediaries, and revenue enforcement.

questions.correctAnswerText: It linked military authority, local intermediaries, and revenue enforcement.
questions.explanation: The letter connects forts, cavalry captains, village headmen, revenue, and ministerial oversight.

questions.prompt: Described Oyo palace wall panel, West Africa, c. 1700: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas while cavalry figures ride beside tribute bearers carrying cloth, beads, and calabashes. A road symbol leads south toward forest towns and coastal brokers.” Which interpretation is best supported by the panel?

choices.A: Oyo rulers rejected cavalry and tribute systems.
choices.B: Coastal brokers ruled Oyo directly.
choices.C: Oyo’s political authority combined royal display, cavalry power, tribute, and regional exchange.
choices.D: Oyo was isolated from inland and coastal routes.

questions.correctAnswerText: Oyo’s political authority combined royal display, cavalry power, tribute, and regional exchange.
questions.explanation: The panel shows royal umbrellas, cavalry, tribute bearers, goods, and routes to brokers.

questions.prompt: Described Oyo palace wall panel, West Africa, c. 1700: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas while cavalry figures ride beside tribute bearers carrying cloth, beads, and calabashes. A road symbol leads south toward forest towns and coastal brokers.” Which broader context best explains the reference to coastal brokers?

choices.A: Inland West African states could be connected indirectly to Atlantic trade through coastal intermediaries.
choices.B: Oyo was founded by Portuguese settlers as a coastal colony.
choices.C: Coastal commerce eliminated all inland states after 1500.
choices.D: Atlantic trade prevented the movement of beads and cloth.

questions.correctAnswerText: Inland West African states could be connected indirectly to Atlantic trade through coastal intermediaries.
questions.explanation: The road toward forest towns and coastal brokers indicates indirect connections to wider Atlantic commerce.

questions.prompt: Described Oyo palace wall panel, West Africa, c. 1700: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas while cavalry figures ride beside tribute bearers carrying cloth, beads, and calabashes. A road symbol leads south toward forest towns and coastal brokers.” Which detail most directly supports the claim that political hierarchy was visually emphasized?

choices.A: “Coastal brokers”
choices.B: “Road symbol”
choices.C: “Cloth, beads, and calabashes”
choices.D: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas”

questions.correctAnswerText: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas”
questions.explanation: Royal umbrellas visually mark the ruler’s high status and political hierarchy.

questions.prompt: Described Oyo palace wall panel, West Africa, c. 1700: “The alaafin sits beneath royal umbrellas while cavalry figures ride beside tribute bearers carrying cloth, beads, and calabashes. A road symbol leads south toward forest towns and coastal brokers.” The panel most directly illustrates which broader pattern?

choices.A: Atlantic exchange always destroyed inland African political authority immediately.
choices.B: Inland African states could adapt regional systems of tribute and power to changing commercial connections.
choices.C: West African rulers had no access to symbolic art.
choices.D: Coastal brokers eliminated all tribute obligations in the interior.

questions.correctAnswerText: Inland African states could adapt regional systems of tribute and power to changing commercial connections.
questions.explanation: The panel links royal authority, tribute, cavalry, and routes toward coastal brokers.

questions.prompt: Originalized Jesuit report from Paraguay, c. 1700: “Guaraní families gather in towns where bells call them to prayer and labor. The mission keeps cattle, teaches music, and claims to protect converts from slave raiders, but the governor asks whether priests command too many people without royal officers.” What was the most likely purpose of the report?

choices.A: To promote the complete abandonment of Catholic missions
choices.B: To describe missionary organization and raise concerns about authority over Indigenous communities
choices.C: To prove that Guaraní communities had no role in mission life
choices.D: To request that all missions become independent republics

questions.correctAnswerText: To describe missionary organization and raise concerns about authority over Indigenous communities
questions.explanation: The report describes mission towns, prayer, labor, protection claims, and the governor’s concern over priestly authority.

questions.prompt: Originalized Jesuit report from Paraguay, c. 1700: “Guaraní families gather in towns where bells call them to prayer and labor. The mission keeps cattle, teaches music, and claims to protect converts from slave raiders, but the governor asks whether priests command too many people without royal officers.” Which phrase best supports the claim that missions sought cultural transformation?

choices.A: “Teaches music”
choices.B: “The governor asks”
choices.C: “Keeps cattle”
choices.D: “Slave raiders”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Teaches music”
questions.explanation: Teaching music reflects mission efforts to reshape cultural and religious practices.

questions.prompt: Originalized Jesuit report from Paraguay, c. 1700: “Guaraní families gather in towns where bells call them to prayer and labor. The mission keeps cattle, teaches music, and claims to protect converts from slave raiders, but the governor asks whether priests command too many people without royal officers.” Which broader context best explains the reference to slave raiders?

choices.A: European monarchs abolished all coercive labor in South America before 1500.
choices.B: Mission towns were unrelated to colonial labor systems.
choices.C: Indigenous communities in colonial frontiers faced pressures from enslavement, resettlement, and missionary control.
choices.D: Paraguay was isolated from Spanish and Portuguese colonial activity.

questions.correctAnswerText: Indigenous communities in colonial frontiers faced pressures from enslavement, resettlement, and missionary control.
questions.explanation: The report references slave raiders, mission towns, prayer, labor, and authority over converts.

questions.prompt: Originalized Jesuit report from Paraguay, c. 1700: “Guaraní families gather in towns where bells call them to prayer and labor. The mission keeps cattle, teaches music, and claims to protect converts from slave raiders, but the governor asks whether priests command too many people without royal officers.” Which statement best evaluates the report’s argument?

choices.A: It presents missions as spaces free from all colonial power.
choices.B: It claims that royal officials controlled every aspect of mission life.
choices.C: It denies that religion shaped Indigenous resettlement.
choices.D: It shows that missionary protection, labor organization, and political authority could be closely connected.

questions.correctAnswerText: It shows that missionary protection, labor organization, and political authority could be closely connected.
questions.explanation: The mission claims protection but also organizes labor and concentrates people under priestly authority.

questions.prompt: Originalized Taiping proclamation, southern China, c. 1853: “The old order worships idols and lets landlords devour the poor. Heaven commands equal fields, moral discipline, and a new kingdom where men and women labor for righteousness.” What was the most likely purpose of the proclamation?

choices.A: To defend Qing rule and landlord privilege
choices.B: To promote European colonial government in China
choices.C: To mobilize support for a religiously inspired social and political rebellion
choices.D: To abolish all land reform proposals

questions.correctAnswerText: To mobilize support for a religiously inspired social and political rebellion
questions.explanation: The proclamation attacks the old order, landlords, and idols while calling for a new kingdom and equal fields.

questions.prompt: Originalized Taiping proclamation, southern China, c. 1853: “The old order worships idols and lets landlords devour the poor. Heaven commands equal fields, moral discipline, and a new kingdom where men and women labor for righteousness.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the proclamation challenged social inequality?

choices.A: “Worships idols”
choices.B: “Landlords devour the poor”
choices.C: “Heaven commands”
choices.D: “Moral discipline”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Landlords devour the poor”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly criticizes social and economic inequality.

questions.prompt: Originalized Taiping proclamation, southern China, c. 1853: “The old order worships idols and lets landlords devour the poor. Heaven commands equal fields, moral discipline, and a new kingdom where men and women labor for righteousness.” Which broader context best explains the proclamation?

choices.A: Nineteenth-century China experienced internal rebellion, social strain, and challenges to Qing legitimacy.
choices.B: China had no major internal conflicts after 1750.
choices.C: Taiping leaders sought to restore Mongol rule over China.
choices.D: The proclamation was part of Cold War land reform in the 1950s.

questions.correctAnswerText: Nineteenth-century China experienced internal rebellion, social strain, and challenges to Qing legitimacy.
questions.explanation: The Taiping movement emerged from social, economic, religious, and political pressures on Qing rule.

questions.prompt: Originalized Taiping proclamation, southern China, c. 1853: “The old order worships idols and lets landlords devour the poor. Heaven commands equal fields, moral discipline, and a new kingdom where men and women labor for righteousness.” Which statement best evaluates the proclamation’s argument?

choices.A: It separates religion from politics entirely.
choices.B: It argues that landlord power is necessary for moral order.
choices.C: It defends Qing social hierarchy as divinely approved.
choices.D: It uses religious language to justify social reform and rebellion against the existing order.

questions.correctAnswerText: It uses religious language to justify social reform and rebellion against the existing order.
questions.explanation: The proclamation invokes Heaven, moral discipline, equal fields, and a new kingdom.

questions.prompt: Originalized socialist workers’ circular, central Europe, c. 1870: “The factory bell divides our lives, while owners speak of freedom in contracts written by hunger. Workers must organize across borders, for steam and capital do not respect the old frontiers.” Which audience was the circular most likely intended to influence?

choices.A: Industrial workers and labor organizers responding to capitalism
choices.B: Buddhist monks managing temple estates
choices.C: Spanish viceroys recording tribute villages
choices.D: Merchants enforcing Portuguese naval licenses

questions.correctAnswerText: Industrial workers and labor organizers responding to capitalism
questions.explanation: The circular addresses factory life, owners, contracts, workers, organization, and capital.

questions.prompt: Originalized socialist workers’ circular, central Europe, c. 1870: “The factory bell divides our lives, while owners speak of freedom in contracts written by hunger. Workers must organize across borders, for steam and capital do not respect the old frontiers.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the author criticized unequal labor relations?

choices.A: “Across borders”
choices.B: “Steam and capital”
choices.C: “Contracts written by hunger”
choices.D: “Old frontiers”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Contracts written by hunger”
questions.explanation: This phrase criticizes supposedly free contracts made under economic pressure.

questions.prompt: Originalized socialist workers’ circular, central Europe, c. 1870: “The factory bell divides our lives, while owners speak of freedom in contracts written by hunger. Workers must organize across borders, for steam and capital do not respect the old frontiers.” The reference to “steam and capital” most directly connects the circular to which development?

choices.A: The decline of mechanized production in Europe
choices.B: The rise of industrial capitalism and mechanized factory labor
choices.C: The replacement of factories by plantation slavery
choices.D: The disappearance of wage labor after 1750

questions.correctAnswerText: The rise of industrial capitalism and mechanized factory labor
questions.explanation: Steam and capital refer to mechanized production and capitalist factory systems.

questions.prompt: Originalized socialist workers’ circular, central Europe, c. 1870: “The factory bell divides our lives, while owners speak of freedom in contracts written by hunger. Workers must organize across borders, for steam and capital do not respect the old frontiers.” Which statement best evaluates the circular’s argument?

choices.A: It argues that industrial laborers should avoid collective action.
choices.B: It claims that factory contracts guaranteed equality between workers and owners.
choices.C: It supports abolishing all communication among workers.
choices.D: It frames labor organization as a transnational response to industrial capitalism.

questions.correctAnswerText: It frames labor organization as a transnational response to industrial capitalism.
questions.explanation: The circular urges workers to organize across borders because capital crosses frontiers.

questions.prompt:
Table: Tunisia and European economic pressure, simplified pattern
Period | Fiscal or political development
1860s | Foreign loans increase under reforming rulers
1870s | International debt commission gains influence over finances
1881 | French protectorate established after diplomatic and military pressure
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Tunisia avoided foreign financial influence in the nineteenth century.
choices.B: Debt and fiscal oversight could become pathways to imperial control.
choices.C: French influence declined as loans increased.
choices.D: Protectorate rule began before debt pressure appeared.

questions.correctAnswerText: Debt and fiscal oversight could become pathways to imperial control.
questions.explanation: The table moves from loans to debt commission influence to a French protectorate.

questions.prompt:
Table: Tunisia and European economic pressure, simplified pattern
Period | Fiscal or political development
1860s | Foreign loans increase under reforming rulers
1870s | International debt commission gains influence over finances
1881 | French protectorate established after diplomatic and military pressure
Which evidence best supports the claim that economic control preceded formal political control?

choices.A: “International debt commission gains influence over finances”
choices.C: “French protectorate”
choices.D: “Reforming rulers”

questions.correctAnswerText: “International debt commission gains influence over finances”
questions.explanation: This evidence shows economic oversight before formal protectorate rule.

questions.prompt:
Table: Tunisia and European economic pressure, simplified pattern
Period | Fiscal or political development
1860s | Foreign loans increase under reforming rulers
1870s | International debt commission gains influence over finances
1881 | French protectorate established after diplomatic and military pressure
Which broader context best explains the pattern shown?

choices.A: European states abandoned North Africa during the age of imperialism.
choices.B: Industrial-era imperialism occurred only through settler migration.
choices.C: North African rulers avoided all borrowing and reform after 1750.
choices.D: European powers used finance, diplomacy, and military pressure to expand influence.

questions.correctAnswerText: European powers used finance, diplomacy, and military pressure to expand influence.
questions.explanation: Loans, debt oversight, diplomatic pressure, and military pressure all contributed to imperial expansion.

questions.prompt:
Table: Tunisia and European economic pressure, simplified pattern
Period | Fiscal or political development
1860s | Foreign loans increase under reforming rulers
1870s | International debt commission gains influence over finances
1881 | French protectorate established after diplomatic and military pressure
Which argument is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Imperialism always began with mass European settlement.
choices.B: Debt commissions strengthened Tunisian sovereignty.
choices.C: Informal economic influence could develop into formal imperial rule.
choices.D: Foreign loans prevented diplomatic intervention.

questions.correctAnswerText: Informal economic influence could develop into formal imperial rule.
questions.explanation: Tunisia’s debt oversight and later protectorate illustrate a path from finance to formal control.

questions.prompt: Described battlefield sketch, southern Africa, 1879: “The image shows Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles, and hills surrounding the battlefield. A caption notes that messengers carried orders between dispersed units.” Which interpretation is best supported by the sketch?

choices.A: Zulu armies relied only on European commanders.
choices.B: British forces fought without firearms.
choices.C: African military organization and local terrain shaped resistance to imperial armies.
choices.D: The battle occurred without communication or tactical planning.

questions.correctAnswerText: African military organization and local terrain shaped resistance to imperial armies.
questions.explanation: The sketch shows Zulu tactical formation, messengers, terrain, and British rifles.

questions.prompt: Described battlefield sketch, southern Africa, 1879: “The image shows Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles, and hills surrounding the battlefield. A caption notes that messengers carried orders between dispersed units.” Which detail best supports the claim that different military technologies and tactics met on the battlefield?

choices.A: “Hills surrounding the battlefield”
choices.B: “Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles”
choices.C: “Messengers carried orders”
choices.D: “Southern Africa”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles”
questions.explanation: This detail directly contrasts military tactics and technologies.

questions.prompt: Described battlefield sketch, southern Africa, 1879: “The image shows Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles, and hills surrounding the battlefield. A caption notes that messengers carried orders between dispersed units.” The sketch most directly connects military resistance to which factor?

choices.A: Knowledge of terrain and coordinated movement in a local environment
choices.B: The absence of African political organization
choices.C: The use of steam-powered naval weapons in inland combat
choices.D: The abandonment of tactics by Zulu forces

questions.correctAnswerText: Knowledge of terrain and coordinated movement in a local environment
questions.explanation: Hills, dispersed units, messengers, and formation suggest terrain knowledge and coordination.

questions.prompt: Described battlefield sketch, southern Africa, 1879: “The image shows Zulu regiments advancing in crescent formation with shields and spears, British troops near wagons and rifles, and hills surrounding the battlefield. A caption notes that messengers carried orders between dispersed units.” Which argument is best supported by the sketch?

choices.A: European imperial armies always defeated African armies without difficulty.
choices.B: African resistance depended only on spiritual belief.
choices.C: Imperial warfare involved no tactical adaptation.
choices.D: African states could use organized military systems to challenge industrial-era imperial expansion.

questions.correctAnswerText: African states could use organized military systems to challenge industrial-era imperial expansion.
questions.explanation: The sketch emphasizes organized regiments, tactics, terrain, and communication.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Anatolia and the Aegean c. 1922–1924 shows Greek and Turkish armies moving through western Anatolia, burned towns near the coast, and arrows marking the compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey.” Which conclusion is best supported by the map?

choices.A: The postwar settlement involved nationalist conflict and forced population exchange.
choices.B: The conflict produced no civilian displacement.
choices.C: Religious identity had no role in population transfers.
choices.D: Greek and Turkish states unified into a single empire.

questions.correctAnswerText: The postwar settlement involved nationalist conflict and forced population exchange.
questions.explanation: The map shows armies, burned towns, religiously defined population movements, and forced exchange.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Anatolia and the Aegean c. 1922–1924 shows Greek and Turkish armies moving through western Anatolia, burned towns near the coast, and arrows marking the compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey.” Which map feature best supports the claim that state policy formalized displacement?

choices.A: “Burned towns near the coast”
choices.B: “Western Anatolia”
choices.C: “Greek and Turkish armies”
choices.D: “Compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly shows state-backed forced displacement.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Anatolia and the Aegean c. 1922–1924 shows Greek and Turkish armies moving through western Anatolia, burned towns near the coast, and arrows marking the compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey.” Which broader context best explains the pattern shown?

choices.A: The end of World War I and the breakup of multiethnic empires intensified nationalist border-making.
choices.B: The Cold War divided Germany into occupation zones.
choices.C: The Indian Ocean spice trade created new port cities.
choices.D: The Mongol Empire expanded across the steppe.

questions.correctAnswerText: The end of World War I and the breakup of multiethnic empires intensified nationalist border-making.
questions.explanation: The conflict followed Ottoman collapse and nationalist state-building in Anatolia and the Aegean.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of Anatolia and the Aegean c. 1922–1924 shows Greek and Turkish armies moving through western Anatolia, burned towns near the coast, and arrows marking the compulsory movement of Orthodox Christians to Greece and Muslims to Turkey.” Which statement best evaluates the map’s argument?

choices.A: It shows that population movement was entirely voluntary.
choices.B: It illustrates how nationalist state-building could involve coercive reclassification and displacement of communities.
choices.C: It denies that war affected civilian populations.
choices.D: It proves that multiethnic empires remained stable after World War I.

questions.correctAnswerText: It illustrates how nationalist state-building could involve coercive reclassification and displacement of communities.
questions.explanation: The map shows compulsory movement based on religious identity and new national boundaries.

questions.prompt:
Table: Atlantic convoy warfare, simplified World War II pattern
Period | Challenge | Response
1940–1941 | High shipping losses to submarines | Larger convoy systems
1942–1943 | Submarine attacks spread across the Atlantic | Radar, sonar, and codebreaking improve escort coordination
1944–1945 | Allied shipping capacity increases | German submarine effectiveness declines
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Technological and organizational changes helped reduce submarine threats to Allied shipping.
choices.B: Submarine warfare had no effect on World War II supply lines.
choices.C: Convoy systems disappeared after 1941.
choices.D: Radar and sonar weakened Allied coordination.

questions.correctAnswerText: Technological and organizational changes helped reduce submarine threats to Allied shipping.
questions.explanation: Larger convoys, radar, sonar, codebreaking, and escort coordination reduced submarine effectiveness.

questions.prompt: Originalized international organization statement, 1945: “After two global wars, states must settle disputes by council rather than conquest. Territories not yet self-governing shall be administered with regard for the advancement of their peoples.” What was the most likely purpose of the statement?

choices.A: To abolish all international institutions after World War II
choices.B: To promote collective security and a framework for oversight of dependent territories
choices.C: To restore all colonial empires without discussion
choices.D: To prevent newly independent states from joining world organizations

questions.correctAnswerText: To promote collective security and a framework for oversight of dependent territories
questions.explanation: The statement emphasizes settling disputes by council and administering dependent territories with regard for their peoples.

questions.prompt: Originalized international organization statement, 1945: “After two global wars, states must settle disputes by council rather than conquest. Territories not yet self-governing shall be administered with regard for the advancement of their peoples.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the statement addressed colonial or dependent peoples?

choices.A: “Two global wars”
choices.B: “Settle disputes”
choices.C: “Territories not yet self-governing”
choices.D: “By council”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Territories not yet self-governing”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly refers to colonial or dependent territories.

questions.prompt: Originalized international organization statement, 1945: “After two global wars, states must settle disputes by council rather than conquest. Territories not yet self-governing shall be administered with regard for the advancement of their peoples.” The statement most directly reflects which post-1945 development?

choices.A: The disappearance of international law after World War II
choices.B: The end of all colonial rule in 1945
choices.C: The restoration of private chartered companies as global governments
choices.D: The creation of global institutions that linked peacekeeping with debates over self-government

questions.correctAnswerText: The creation of global institutions that linked peacekeeping with debates over self-government
questions.explanation: The statement reflects post-1945 international organizations addressing both peace and dependent territories.

questions.prompt:
Table: Cold War space activity, simplified pattern
Period | Development
1957–1961 | First satellites and human spaceflight demonstrate superpower capabilities
1960s | Space technology supports communications, weather observation, and military surveillance
1970s | Cooperative space projects occur alongside competition
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Space activity was unrelated to Cold War politics.
choices.B: Space programs served only scientific purposes without military or ideological value.
choices.C: Space technology became a field of superpower competition and practical global applications.
choices.D: Space cooperation ended all rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union.

questions.correctAnswerText: Space technology became a field of superpower competition and practical global applications.
questions.explanation: The table shows satellites and human spaceflight, communications, weather observation, surveillance, and cooperation.

questions.prompt:
Table: Cold War space activity, simplified pattern
Period | Development
1957–1961 | First satellites and human spaceflight demonstrate superpower capabilities
1960s | Space technology supports communications, weather observation, and military surveillance
1970s | Cooperative space projects occur alongside competition
Which argument is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Cold War rivalry could encourage technological innovation while also producing limited cooperation.
choices.B: Space technology eliminated ideological competition after 1957.
choices.C: Satellites had no influence on communication or surveillance.
choices.D: Space activity was limited to one country.

questions.correctAnswerText: Cold War rivalry could encourage technological innovation while also producing limited cooperation.
questions.explanation: Space programs involved competition, practical applications, surveillance, and cooperative projects.

questions.prompt:
Table: Renewable energy and critical minerals, simplified global pattern
Indicator | c. 2000 | c. 2020
Solar and wind capacity | Limited in most regions | Rapidly expanded in many regions
Demand for lithium, cobalt, and rare earths | Lower | Higher because of batteries and electronics
Supply-chain concern | Mostly specialized industry issue | Wider political and environmental debate
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Renewable energy reduced all demand for minerals.
choices.B: Energy transitions created new global supply-chain and environmental debates.
choices.C: Solar and wind capacity declined after 2000.
choices.D: Critical minerals became irrelevant to electronics.

questions.correctAnswerText: Energy transitions created new global supply-chain and environmental debates.
questions.explanation: Renewable expansion increased demand for critical minerals and broadened supply-chain concerns.

questions.prompt:
Table: Renewable energy and critical minerals, simplified global pattern
Indicator | c. 2000 | c. 2020
Solar and wind capacity | Limited in most regions | Rapidly expanded in many regions
Demand for lithium, cobalt, and rare earths | Lower | Higher because of batteries and electronics
Supply-chain concern | Mostly specialized industry issue | Wider political and environmental debate
The pattern shown most directly contributed to which development?

choices.A: The end of global commodity markets
choices.B: Increased competition over mineral extraction, processing, and technology supply chains
choices.C: The disappearance of environmental regulation
choices.D: The return to exclusively coal-powered industry

questions.correctAnswerText: Increased competition over mineral extraction, processing, and technology supply chains
questions.explanation: Higher mineral demand for batteries and electronics increased competition over extraction and supply chains.

questions.prompt: Originalized statement by an Indigenous community representative, c. 2020: “The world calls the battery clean, but the mine drinks our water and scars our grazing land. We do not reject new energy; we demand consent, repair, and a share in decisions made far from our desert.” What was the most likely purpose of the statement?

choices.A: To deny that energy transitions affect local environments
choices.B: To argue that renewable-energy supply chains should address local rights and environmental costs
choices.C: To support mining without regulation or community input
choices.D: To claim that batteries are unrelated to global markets

questions.correctAnswerText: To argue that renewable-energy supply chains should address local rights and environmental costs
questions.explanation: The statement demands consent, repair, local decision-making, and recognition of environmental costs.

questions.prompt: Originalized statement by an Indigenous community representative, c. 2020: “The world calls the battery clean, but the mine drinks our water and scars our grazing land. We do not reject new energy; we demand consent, repair, and a share in decisions made far from our desert.” Which phrase best supports the claim that local communities challenged decision-making from distant centers?

choices.A: “The battery clean”
choices.B: “Grazing land”
choices.C: “A share in decisions made far from our desert”
choices.D: “New energy”

questions.correctAnswerText: “A share in decisions made far from our desert”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly challenges distant decision-making that affects local communities.

questions.prompt: Originalized statement by an Indigenous community representative, c. 2020: “The world calls the battery clean, but the mine drinks our water and scars our grazing land. We do not reject new energy; we demand consent, repair, and a share in decisions made far from our desert.” Which statement best evaluates the argument?

choices.A: It frames globalization’s energy transition as requiring both technological change and attention to environmental justice.
choices.B: It rejects all renewable-energy technology as impossible.
choices.C: It claims that mining has no social consequences.
choices.D: It separates global energy markets from local land rights.

questions.correctAnswerText: It frames globalization’s energy transition as requiring both technological change and attention to environmental justice.
questions.explanation: The statement supports new energy while demanding consent, repair, and community participation.
